product_updates.title: OpenAI and Google employees rush to Anthropic’s defense in DOD lawsuit
product_updates.content: More than 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees signed onto a statement supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the Defense Department after the agency labeled the AI firm a supply-chain risk, according to court filings.

product_updates.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
product_updates.content: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI.

product_updates.title: OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
product_updates.content: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

product_updates.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply-chain risk designation
product_updates.content: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense on Monday after the agency labeled it a supply-chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful."

product_updates.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
product_updates.content: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

product_updates.title: Sandberg, Clegg join Nscale board as this ‘Stargate Norway’ startup hits $14.6B valuation
product_updates.content: Nvidia-backed British AI infrastructure startup Nscale has raised another megaround of $2 billion.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The usability imperative for securing digital asset devices
technical_breakthroughs.content: When Tony Fadell started working on the iPod, usability often trumped security. The result was an iterative process.
